title: OpenThorn vs Lovable
description: Lovable charges $25+/month for credits; OpenThorn is free and you pay your AI provider directly. An honest comparison of pricing, model choice, and code ownership.
intro: Lovable is one of the most popular AI app builders — and a genuinely good product. The fundamental difference is the business model: Lovable resells AI usage as monthly credits, while OpenThorn is BYOK — you bring an API key from any of 17 providers and pay that provider's raw per-token rates. No subscription, no markup.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Credits per edit (~0.5–1.2+ credits each), bought from Lovable

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere
Whole platform is free; you only pay provider API usage

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Lovable?
faqs.answer: Yes. The OpenThorn platform is completely free — you connect your own AI provider API key and pay only that provider's per-token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per website. Lovable requires a subscription from $25/month for meaningful usage.

faqs.question: Can I use my own OpenAI or Anthropic API key with Lovable?
faqs.answer: No. As of June 2026 Lovable does not support bring-your-own-key; AI usage is billed in Lovable credits. OpenThorn is built entirely around BYOK with 17 supported providers.

faqs.question: Which is cheaper, OpenThorn or Lovable?
faqs.answer: For most usage, OpenThorn — you pay raw provider token prices with no markup or subscription. A typical website costs cents to a few dollars in API usage versus a $25+/month Lovable subscription. Lovable can be simpler if you prefer one predictable monthly bill.

verdict: Choose Lovable if you want a polished all-in-one subscription and don't want to think about API keys. Choose OpenThorn if you want to pick your own models, pay raw token prices with no subscription, and keep full ownership of the exported code.

title: OpenThorn vs Bolt.new
description: Bolt.new sells AI tokens in monthly packs from $25/month; OpenThorn is free and BYOK — you pay your AI provider directly. Pricing, model choice, and ownership compared.
intro: Bolt.new (by StackBlitz) is a strong in-browser AI builder. Like Lovable it resells AI usage — you buy token packs from Bolt. OpenThorn takes the BYOK route: connect your own provider key and pay raw per-token rates with no subscription. Bolt's own team also maintains bolt.diy, an open-source BYOK variant — which says something about the demand for this model.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Token packs from Bolt (~10–13M tokens/month on Pro)
No (the separate open-source bolt.diy supports it, self-hosted)

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
Whole platform is free; you only pay provider API usage
Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Bolt.new?
faqs.answer: Yes. OpenThorn charges nothing — you connect your own provider API key and pay raw token rates. Bolt.new's free tier is capped at 1M tokens/month (300K/day); meaningful usage needs the $25/month Pro plan.

faqs.question: Can I use my own API key with Bolt.new?
faqs.answer: Not with the hosted Bolt.new — it bills in its own token packs. The open-source bolt.diy project supports BYOK but you must run it yourself. OpenThorn is hosted, free, and BYOK out of the box.

faqs.question: What is the difference between buying Bolt tokens and BYOK?
faqs.answer: Bolt sells you a monthly allotment of tokens it buys from providers. With BYOK you skip the middleman: your key, your provider's prices, your spend limits, and unused budget simply stays unspent — there is no monthly pack to use up.

verdict: Choose Bolt.new for its polished StackBlitz-powered dev environment and a single monthly bill. Choose OpenThorn to pay raw provider prices with no subscription — or bolt.diy if you want open source and are happy self-hosting.

title: OpenThorn vs v0 (Vercel)
description: v0 meters AI usage in credits on plans from $20/month; OpenThorn is free and BYOK. How the two compare on pricing, model choice, scope, and code ownership.
intro: v0 by Vercel is excellent at generating React/Next.js UI and ships tight Vercel integration. It bills AI usage in credits metered over tokens, on plans from $20/month. OpenThorn is a free BYOK website builder: bring a key from any of 17 providers, pay raw token rates, and deploy or export wherever you like.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Credits metered over input/output tokens, bought from Vercel

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
rows.competitor: v0's own model tiers (Mini, Pro, Max)

rows.openthorn: Provider-agnostic; deploy to Netlify or export and host anywhere
rows.competitor: Optimized for Next.js and Vercel deployment

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to v0?
faqs.answer: Yes. OpenThorn's platform is free and you pay your AI provider directly per token. v0's free tier includes about $5/month in credits; sustained use needs Premium at $20/month.

faqs.question: Can I use my own OpenAI or Anthropic key with v0?
faqs.answer: No — v0 bills usage in its own credits as of June 2026. OpenThorn is built around bring-your-own-key with 17 supported providers.

faqs.question: Should I use v0 or OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: If you live in the Vercel/Next.js ecosystem and want best-in-class UI generation with a subscription, v0 is a great fit. If you want a free, provider-agnostic builder where you control the model and pay raw token prices, use OpenThorn.

verdict: v0 is the strongest choice for Next.js teams already on Vercel. OpenThorn is the choice for BYOK: free platform, 17 providers, raw token pricing, and no ecosystem lock-in.
